creator: madpilot@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi,

Thanks for reporting this. Personally I've not been using this functionality for some time, but I'd like not to cut functionality from ported software if not strictly necessary.

I'm taking a look though.

At present this is bundled with the CDDA functionality, which should not be lost, so I can't simply remove that option.

Unluckily upstream does not provide a way to compile CD audio without CDDB. So it is impossible to only disable that part.

Can you elaborate what you mean by "we plan to rip out libcddb in the end"? Is it planned to remove the cddb library from ports? is there an expiration date?

Because if that port is removed the whole CD Audio functionality of audacious will need to be removed.

OTOH lookup failure errors are unpleasant but not a big deal. Also the post you link to states an alternative server is available, I could set that one up as default.
